nameMeanings.name: Mallett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Malot, more rarely Malet, a pet form of Mall , Mald (i.e. Maud, Matilda) + the diminutive suffix -ot or -et. In post-medieval forms of the surname final -ot was generally altered to -et(t). Though there are alternative Norman origins (see below), this is the usual source of the English surname.

English: of Norman origin, a nickname from Old French maillet ‘hammer’ (see Maillet , compare Mallet 4), used as a battle weapon. Robert Malet, lord of the Honour of Eye (Suffolk) in 1086, was the son of William Malet of Graville-Sainte-Honorine (Seine-Maritime). The latter is referred to as Gulielmo agnomine Mal(l)eto ‘William, surnamed Mal(l)et’ in the late 11th-century Gesta Guillelmi of William of Poitiers and the early 12th-century Historia Ecclesia of Orderic Vitalis. According to tradition, this surname arose because of William's reputation for bludgeoning his enemies in battle (cum) malleo ‘with the hammer’. The story may be true or it may have been invented to explain the name, in which case see 3 below for an alternative etymology.
English: of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Malet, a pet form of Malo . Compare Mallet 2.

variants.name: Mall
meanings: English: from the medieval female personal name Mall, a common form of Mald or Maud. See also Maude and Moll . It had several pet forms; see Malin , Malkin , and Mallet .

variants.name: Maillet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Yvon, Emile, Ulysse, Alphee, Andre, Jean-Paul, Laurent, Normand, Alban, Alcide, Allain, Antoine.
from Old French maillet ‘mallet’, used as a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked with such an implement, e.g. a smith, or as a nickname for a fearsome warrior (see English Mallett 2).

variants.name: Maillot
meanings: from Old French maillot ‘big mallet’, used as a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked with such an implement, e.g. a smith, and perhaps also as a nickname for a fearsome warrior (see English Mallett 2). This is the usual form of the surname in France (it is, however, most frequent in Réunion), while in North America it is normally spelled Mailhot .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Mallett was born in 1414, in Bridgwater, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hugh Malet, was 25 and his mother, Joan Roynon, was 13. He had at least 1 daughter with Judeth.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Mallett est né en 1414, à Bridgwater, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh Malet, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan Roynon, avait 13 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Judeth.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Mallett 1414 in Bridgwater, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh Malet 25 und seine Mutter Joan Roynon 13. Er hatte mit Judeth mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Mallett è nato il nel 1414 nel Bridgwater, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hugh Malet, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Joan Roynon, aveva 13 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Judeth.

ko.lifeSummary: William Mallett(이)가 1414년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 브리지왓어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh Malet(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Roynon(은)는 13세였다. 그는 Judeth(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Mallett nasceu em 1414, em Bridgwater, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hugh Malet, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Joan Roynon, tinha 13 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Judeth.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Mallett родился в 1414 г. в местности бриджвэитир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hugh Malet было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Roynon, 13. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Judeth.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Mallett nació en 1414, en Bridgwater, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Hugh Malet, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Joan Roynon, tenía 13 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Judeth.
